commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `--global` to `openclaw skills install` and `openclaw skills update`, targets `CONFIG_DIR/skills`, rejects `--global` with `--agent`, and updates skills docs/tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a new CLI feature request. Source inspection confirms current main lacks `--global` while the shared managed skills root already exists and is loaded.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer acceptance and normal merge validation for a new user-facing CLI option, not a ClawSweeper repair.

target.title: feat(cli): support installing skills to shared global directory via --global
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw skills install` only targets workspace directories, requiring manual file movement for shared skills
- Why it matters: Multi-agent setups need skills shared globally across agents
- What changed: Added `--global` flag to `install` and `update` commands, targeting `~/.openclaw/skills`
- What did NOT change: Default behavior unchanged; workspace resolution logic untouched; ClawHub installer unchanged

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #74124
- Related #56161
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A (feature request)

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test file: `src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - `--global` installs to CONFIG_DIR
  - `--global` and `--agent` mutually exclusive
  - `--global` works for both install and update
- 6 new unit tests added

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New `--global` flag for `openclaw skills install` and `openclaw skills update`
- `--global` installs/updates skills in `~/.openclaw/skills` instead of workspace

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15.7.4
- Runtime/container: Node.js v25.6.1
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: CLI

### Steps

1. `openclaw skills install weather --global`
2. Check `~/.openclaw/skills/weather` exists
3. `openclaw skills update weather --global`
4. Verify update detects already-at-latest
5. `openclaw skills install weather --global --agent main` → error (mutual exclusion)

### Expected

- Skill installed/updated in shared directory `~/.openclaw/skills/`
- `--global` + `--agent` rejected with clear error

### Actual

- All scenarios work as expected (see proof below)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

Unit tests:
```
pnpm test src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts
# 27 tests passed (6 new)
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Unit tests pass (27/27), manual E2E with real ClawHub install and update
- Edge cases checked: `--global` + `--agent` conflict, parent `--agent` with child `--global`
- What you did NOT verify: N/A (all scenarios verified)

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `--global` flag for `openclaw skills install` and `openclaw skills update` installs/updates skills in shared `~/.openclaw/skills/` directory
- **Real environment tested**: macOS 15.7.4, Node v25.6.1, running from source checkout via `npx tsx src/entry.ts`
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch**:
  1. `openclaw skills install weather --global`
  2. `openclaw skills update weather --global`
  3. `openclaw skills install weather --global --agent main`
  4. `openclaw skills install -h`
- **Evidence after fix**:

```
$ openclaw skills install weather --global
Downloading weather@1.0.0 from ClawHub…
Installing to ~/.openclaw/skills/weather…
Installed weather@1.0.0 -> ~/.openclaw/skills/weather

$ openclaw skills update weather --global
Downloading weather@1.0.0 from ClawHub…
Installing to ~/.openclaw/skills/weather…
weather already at 1.0.0

$ openclaw skills install weather --global --agent main
Use either --global or --agent, not both.
(exit code 1)

$ openclaw skills install -h
Usage: openclaw skills install [options] <slug>

Install a skill from ClawHub into the active or shared managed directory

Options:
  --agent <id>         Target agent workspace
  --force              Overwrite an existing workspace skill (default: false)
  --global             Install into the shared managed skills directory (default: false)
  --version <version>  Install a specific version
  -h, --help           Display help for command
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All four scenarios work as expected — install to global dir, update detects already-at-latest, mutual exclusion rejects with clear error, help shows `--global` option
- **What was not tested**: None

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Global installs may override workspace-specific versions
  - Mitigation: Workspace skills have higher precedence in skill loader (documented behavior)
